instructions.instruction: Run bash scripts/validate.sh <script-path> as the first step before analyzing any script. Never skip this step even if the script looks correct at a glance.
instructions.relevant_when: User asks to validate, review, check, or audit any shell script
instructions.why_given: The validator catches classes of issues (word splitting, POSIX violations, command injection) that are invisible to casual reading.

instructions.instruction: For each issue found, show: (a) the problematic code snippet, (b) the issue explanation with ShellCheck code if applicable, (c) the corrected code, (d) why the fix improves the script.
instructions.relevant_when: Reporting validation results to the user
instructions.why_given: The structured four-part format ensures the developer understands the root cause, not just the symptom.

instructions.instruction: Treat all errors (exit code 2) as blocking. Do not declare a script valid if errors exist. Warnings (exit code 1) should be fixed but may be explained if a suppression is intentional.
instructions.relevant_when: Deciding whether a script passes validation
instructions.why_given: Errors indicate syntax problems or unsafe constructs that will cause runtime failures; warnings indicate likely bugs.

instructions.instruction: Never add a global # shellcheck disable=SCxxxx directive at the top of a file. When suppression is truly needed, add it on the specific line with an explanatory comment.
instructions.relevant_when: Applying ShellCheck suppressions to a script
instructions.why_given: File-level suppressions hide real issues added by future contributors who may not know why the suppression was added.

instructions.instruction: Treat #!/bin/sh and #!/usr/bin/env bash shebangs as distinct. A #!/bin/sh script must not use bash-specific syntax (arrays, [[ ]], declare, local without -r). A #!/usr/bin/env bash script must not omit the shebang.
instructions.relevant_when: Validating any shell script's shebang line and syntax
instructions.why_given: Using bash syntax under a sh shebang silently fails on Alpine Linux, minimal containers, and most CI runners.

instructions.instruction: Flag any use of eval with a variable as a potential command injection risk. Flag rm -rf calls that operate on unquoted or insufficiently validated variables.
instructions.relevant_when: Running custom security checks during validation
instructions.why_given: These are the two highest-severity security anti-patterns in shell scripts and require explicit human review.

instructions.instruction: Never use exit 0 or || true inside a function to suppress errors. Functions should return meaningful exit codes so that callers and set -e can detect failures.
instructions.relevant_when: Reviewing or fixing error handling in shell scripts
instructions.why_given: Suppressing exit codes silently hides failures, making debugging non-deterministic.
